decisions.rationale: The gap contains only a comma and whitespace, serving as a syntactic separator without independent assertive content.
The gap consists of the conjunction 'and' with surrounding whitespace, functioning solely to link two claim spans without adding new semantic assertions.
The gap contains ', whereas ', which acts as a contrastive connector between clauses but does not itself constitute an independent claim.
The gap is the conjunction 'and' with whitespace, linking causal outcomes without introducing new propositional content.
The gap is a single period marking the end of the sentence, containing no assertive semantic content.
The gap contains ' and ', serving as a coordinator between two prediction observable claims without independent meaning.
The gap is a terminal period, which is punctuation only and carries no claim-bearing content.
The gap contains ', and ', functioning as a syntactic bridge between two success criteria propositions.
The gap is a period ending the sentence, lacking any independent semantic assertion.
The gap is the conjunction 'and' with whitespace, linking two parts of the success criterion without adding new claims.
The gap is a single period, serving only as sentence-ending punctuation.
The gap contains ' and ', connecting two falsification observables without contributing independent assertive content.
The gap contains ', despite ', which introduces a concessive clause but is itself a connector rather than an independent claim.
The gap is a terminal period, containing no semantic content beyond sentence demarcation.
